methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Diode is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Davide Asnaghi and Lenny Khazan that has raised $10M+. The comparable universe for Robotics & Manufacturing includes one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, notably 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable and anchors the analysis. Using total capital raised of $10M as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $25M. This method captures the expected step-function appreciation between funding rounds in early-stage venture markets. The peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; while eleven robotics peers exist in the broader sector, none meet the same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier filters simultaneously. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and business models.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a range of $21M (low) to $29M (high), with a median of $25M. This relatively tight band reflects reliance on a single valuation driver rather than methodological consensus. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable seed-stage robotics transaction data and Diode's specific positioning within the sector, which could justify material variance from typical step-up multiples depending on traction, IP, and market opportunity.
